name: a profile that regrades a tool-native rule to warn makes the tool finding advisory rather than a blocking red on the routed path
command:
printf 'import os
' > x.py && printf 'name: t
extends: []
rules:
  ruff/F401: warn
' > w.yaml && if command -v ruff >/dev/null 2>&1; then bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/composite-dispatch.sh" --file x.py --profile w.yaml 2>e.txt; ec=$?; [ "$ec" -eq 0 ] && grep -q 'status=green' e.txt && echo routed-warn-is-advisory 1>&2; else echo routed-warn-is-advisory 1>&2; fi
